programs.name: Utah Medicaid Aging Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Provides home and community-based services as an alternative to nursing home care for eligible seniors. Services include homemaking, personal care, and respite care based on individual care plans.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65 and older who meet income/assets eligibility and professional risk assessment.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by care plan and provider availability; covers long-term care services to delay institutionalization.[4]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings. Integrates Medicare and Medicaid benefits for coordinated care.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ who are nursing facility eligible and live in a PACE service area (limited availability in Utah).
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive care including adult day health, transportation, and home care at little to no cost for dual eligibles.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Helps pay Medicare Part A and B premiums, deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments for low-income beneficiaries. Administered through Utah Medicaid as Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), and Qualifying Individual (QI).[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries meeting specific income and resource limits.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Premium and cost-sharing assistance up to program maximums based on income level.[5]

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card to purchase food for low-income households. Helps seniors afford nutritious meals.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors and households meeting federal income guidelines.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by household size and income; average monthly benefit around $200-$300 per person for eligible seniors.

programs.name: LIHEAP (Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Offers help with heating and cooling bills, crisis assistance, and weatherization referrals for low-income households. Administered statewide in Utah.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors meeting income eligibility.[5]
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants typically $200-$1,000 per household depending on fuel type and need.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Improves energy efficiency in low-income homes through insulation, sealing, and heating system repairs. Reduces energy costs for seniors.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income homeowners or renters aged 60+ prioritized in Utah.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Free home upgrades valued at $5,000-$10,000 per household.

programs.name: Utah State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free counseling on Medicare options, appeals, and extra help programs. Helps seniors navigate benefits.[2]
programs.who_its_for: All Utah Medicare beneficiaries, especially low-income seniors.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Free personalized counseling and application assistance.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels (Home-Delivered Meals)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote independence. Often provided through local Area Agencies on Aging.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors aged 60+ who are unable to prepare meals.[4]
programs.benefit_value: 5-7 meals per week; suggested donation $3-5 per meal, free for very low-income.

programs.name: Utah Caregiver Support Program (UCSP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers respite care, counseling, training, and support to family caregivers. Includes temporary relief to delay facility placement.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid family caregivers of adults 60+, those with Alzheimer's, or older relatives 55+ caring for children.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Varies; respite up to 240 hours/year, no income requirement.[4]

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides community service work training and employment for low-income older workers. Pays minimum wage for part-time work.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage for 20 hours/week training positions leading to employment.

programs.name: Legal Services for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Offers free legal aid for issues like housing, benefits, and abuse for low-income seniors. Provided through Utah Legal Services and Area Agencies on Aging.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Free legal representation and advice for eligible cases.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care. Operated through Utah's Aging and Adult Services.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and other long-term care facilities.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy services resolving over 90% of complaints.
